extractions.problem: Oncogenic miRNA-21 is upregulated in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), and targeted delivery of siRNA to inhibit miRNA-21 in EGFR-overexpressing TNBC cells remains a challenge.
extractions.method: In-silico molecular docking (Autodock Vina, I-TASSER) for siRNA-miRNA-21 and GE11-EGFR binding, combined with in vitro studies (MTT, flow cytometry, qPCR, western blot) on chitosan nanoparticle formulations.
extractions.finding: GE11-siRNA-CSNPs showed enhanced cytotoxicity against TNBC cells, significantly inhibited miRNA-21 expression, cell migration, and colony formation, and induced apoptosis by blocking the PI3K/AKT and ERK1/2 signaling axis.
extractions.dataset: MDA-MB-231 (TNBC) and MCF7 breast cancer cell lines
extractions.metric: Binding energy (kcal/mol), cell viability %, gene expression levels, particle size/PDI/zeta potential
extractions.baseline: Untreated cells, naked siRNA-CSNPs (without GE11 peptide conjugation)
extractions.result: siRNA-miRNA-21 binding energy −18.98 kcal/mol, GE11-EGFR binding energy −52.62 kcal/mol, nanoparticle size 122 nm

extractions.problem: Experimentally validated miRNA:gene interactions are fragmented across thousands of manuscripts and raw NGS datasets, making comprehensive access difficult for researchers.
extractions.method: Text-mining pipeline for article selection combined with manual curation of published experiments and analysis of >150 raw NGS datasets (HITS-CLIP, PAR-CLIP, CLASH).
extractions.finding: DIANA-TarBase v7.0 indexed over half a million experimentally validated miRNA:gene interactions across 24 species and 356 cell types, achieving 9- to 250-fold more entries than any other relevant database.
extractions.dataset: MedLine/PubMed Central articles, 150+ raw NGS datasets, 24 species, 356 cell types
extractions.metric: Number of curated interactions, species coverage, cell type coverage
extractions.baseline: DIANA-TarBase v6.0 and other miRNA target databases (miRTarBase, miRecords, StarBase)
extractions.result: >500,000 interactions, 9-250x more entries than competing databases

extractions.problem: During noninvasive prenatal testing (NIPT), cell-free fetal DNA fraction can only be accurately quantified from Y-chromosomal reads for male fetuses, requiring advanced methods for gender-independent prediction.
extractions.method: PREFACE combines principal component analysis with neural networks to model copy number profiles from shallow whole-genome sequencing (sWGS) data, enabling semi-supervised training with both male and unlabeled female samples.
extractions.finding: PREFACE achieved Pearson r=0.94 between predicted and Y-based fetal fractions using the full cohort; the method can also predict mixed multiple pregnancies and sex chromosomal aneuploidies.
extractions.dataset: NIPT cohort (n_female=2468, n_male=2723, total 5572 samples) from Center for Medical Genetics Ghent
extractions.baseline: Elastic net (supervised), SeqFF, FFY (Y-chromosomal read count-based fraction)
extractions.result: Pearson r=0.94 (full cohort), r=0.9 (~1100 males only), superior to elastic net

extractions.problem: No vaccine exists for Chlamydia trachomatis despite it being the most common bacterial STI globally; the conserved PmpD protein is a promising immunogen but its large size and complex structure hinder recombinant approaches.
extractions.method: In silico epitope prediction using NetMHC/NetMHCII (T-cell), BepiPred (linear B-cell), RaptorX (3D homology modeling), and DiscoTope (conformational B-cell epitopes).
extractions.finding: Six regions containing overlapping B-cell and T-cell epitopes were identified by multiple predictors, representing valuable candidates for subunit vaccine design against C. trachomatis.
extractions.dataset: C. trachomatis serovar L2 PmpD amino acid sequence (Genbank AAK69391.2, 1530 residues)
extractions.metric: Binding affinity (strong/weak), P-value, uGDT, Ramachandran plot quality
extractions.result: 70 strong MHC-I epitopes, 742 strong MHC-II epitopes, 24 linear B-cell epitopes, 91% structure modeled, 6 multi-epitope consensus regions

extractions.problem: Gene expression profiling of bulk breast tumors conflates mRNA from tumor epithelial and stromal cells, making it difficult to ascribe expression differences to specific cell types for metastasis prediction.
extractions.method: Weighted t-statistics using histopathological area fraction estimates of tumor and stromal components from H&E-stained sections of 198 fresh-frozen breast tumors, validated against laser capture microdissected tissue.
extractions.finding: Gene expression differences between distant metastasis and no-metastasis groups were mostly ascribed to tumor epithelial cells; PIP5K2A was specifically elevated in stroma of the metastasis group, confirmed in microdissected tissue.
extractions.dataset: 198 fresh-frozen breast tumors (DBCG82b&c cohort: 118 DM, 80 NoDM)
extractions.result: 182 DEGs (unweighted), 30 gene-probes ascribed solely to carcinoma cells, 38 mostly tumor-associated, 5 stromal-associated

extractions.problem: Metastatic breast cancer remains a leading cause of death in women, necessitating the discovery of effective natural-product-based cytotoxic and anti-migration agents.
extractions.method: Ethanol/water extraction, MTT assay for cytotoxicity, scratch wound healing assay for migration inhibition, UHPLC-MS phytochemical profiling, and in silico molecular docking against Bcl-2, Bcl-XL, ERK2, and FAK.
extractions.finding: Ethanol extract of K. parviflora (EEKP) showed dose-dependent cytotoxicity on 4T1 and MCF-7 cells with no toxicity on NIH-3T3; HTMF and denbinobin were identified as responsible bioactive compounds via docking.
extractions.dataset: 4T1 (metastatic breast cancer), MCF-7, NIH-3T3 (non-cancerous) cell lines
extractions.result: IC50 128.33 µg/mL (24h) and 115.09 µg/mL (48h) on 4T1, 138.43/124.81 µg/mL on MCF-7, no cytotoxicity on NIH-3T3 up to 250 µg/mL

extractions.problem: Amyloid-β misfolds into toxic oligomers causing neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease, and preventing toxic oligomer formation is a potential therapeutic strategy.
extractions.method: Molecular dynamics simulations for inhibitor design and optimization, single molecule force spectroscopy (SMFS) for binding validation, and MTT cell viability assays on HT22 mouse hippocampal cells.
extractions.finding: SGA1 and SGA3 (anti-parallel binding pseudopeptides) demonstrated neuroprotection against Aβ(1-42) oligomers, while SGC1 (parallel binding) was not protective; myristoylated variants intended for BBB crossing were cytotoxic.
extractions.dataset: HT22 mouse hippocampal cells, Aβ(1-42) oligomers
extractions.metric: Cell viability %, IC50, one-way ANOVA with Dunnett's test
extractions.baseline: Aβ(1-42) oligomer-treated cells (50±5% viability), untreated control (100%)
extractions.result: SGA1 improved viability to 65±6%, SGA3 to 68±3% (vs 50±5% Aβ alone, p<0.05/0.01), Myr-SGA IC50 ~7.5 µM (cytotoxic)

extractions.problem: In silico prediction of discontinuous B-cell epitopes has shown moderate performance, partly due to improper benchmark definitions that create artificial false positives from incomplete epitope annotations.
extractions.method: DiscoTope-2.0 with novel spatial neighborhood scoring function and half-sphere exposure (HSE) as surface measure, evaluated with proper benchmarks incorporating biological unit and multiple epitope information.
extractions.finding: Proper benchmark definitions significantly improved reported prediction performance; DiscoTope-2.0 outperformed other state-of-the-art methods on both cross-validation and independent evaluation datasets.
extractions.dataset: 75 antigen structures (DiscoTope training set), 52 independent evaluation structures from IEDB-3D (clustered into 33 homology groups)
extractions.baseline: DiscoTope-1.2, PEPITO, ElliPro, SEPPA, Epitopia, EPCES, EPSVR
extractions.result: AUC 0.748 (cross-validation), improved to 0.824 with proper benchmarks; significantly better than Epitopia (p=0.033) and EPSVR (p=0.006)

extractions.problem: Growing cancer incidence and resistance to conventional anti-cancer drugs necessitate the discovery of new chemotherapeutic agents from natural sources, particularly Amaryllidaceae alkaloids.
extractions.method: In vitro cytotoxicity (MTT assay) of 10 alkaloid fractions against 6 cancer cell lines, molecular docking against Bcl-2 family (Mcl-1, Bcl-xL) and HK2, and micropropagation of endangered species via tissue culture.
extractions.finding: C. jagus and E. bonplandii showed strongest activity on AGS gastric cancer cells; lycorine and haemanthamine were the most active pure alkaloids; powelline and buphanidrine identified as promising candidates via combined in vitro/in silico analysis.
extractions.dataset: AGS, BT-549, HEC-1B, MCF-7, MDA-MB-231, PC3 cancer cell lines, HaCat control; 10 Amaryllidaceae species
extractions.metric: Cell viability %, IC50 (µg/mL and µM), binding energy
extractions.baseline: Doxorubicin (positive control, IC50 5.73±0.80 µg/mL on AGS)
extractions.result: Lycorine IC50 4.17±0.18 µg/mL (14.51 µM), haemanthamine IC50 13.18±0.47 µg/mL (43.74 µM) on AGS; E. bonplandii 45.79±3.05% viability at 30 µg/mL

extractions.problem: Vernonia amygdalina is used in traditional African medicine as an antidiabetic remedy, but the specific roles of its bioactive compounds luteolin and vernodalol in antidiabetic and anticancer activity are unclear.
extractions.method: HPLC quantification of luteolin and vernodalol in leaf/root extracts, α-glucosidase inhibition assay, BSA glycation assay, ORAC antioxidant assay, MTT cell viability on HT-29 cells, and in silico ADMET prediction.
extractions.finding: Luteolin (prevalent in leaves) is responsible for antioxidant and antidiabetic effects, while vernodalol (prevalent in roots) drives antiproliferative activity; both compounds have suitable ADMET profiles as drug candidates.
extractions.dataset: HT-29 colon cancer cells, Vernonia amygdalina leaf and root extracts (6 extract types)
extractions.metric: IC50 (µM), ORAC (µmol TE/g), α-glucosidase inhibition %, cell viability %
extractions.result: Luteolin IC50 22.2 µM, vernodalol IC50 5.7 µM on HT-29; luteolin in EthLE 0.54±0.04 mg/g, vernodalol in EthRE 336.79±15.60 mg/g
